title: narcissus sylvestris proles johnstonii
intro: Narcissus sylvestris proles johnstonii, also known as Wild Daffodil, is a perennial plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family. It is a rare and lesser-known variant of the common narcissus, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine, which have been shown to possess neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Wild Daffodil include its potential to improve cognitive function, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate symptoms of depression and insomnia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, although its use is not widely practiced due to concerns over toxicity and potential side effects.
